content.claim: At baseline, approximately 40% of Hungarian primary schools have zero students attempting selective-track entrance exams (60% in village schools), while better schools in county seats and Budapest experience class-size drops of 20-30% by grade 7 as students leave for gimnázium, indicating selective tracks function as urban/regional-center phenomenon with minimal rural uptake.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Data vintage and exact geographic scope not specified in research notes
Rural/urban category boundary definitions may vary by settlement size classification
Mechanism of non-participation (family choice, access barriers, awareness) not differentiated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
